---
title: "Heat trees with metacoder"
author: "Kira Goff"
date: "2026-07-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r clear-workspace}
rm(list = ls(all = TRUE))
```


```{r load-libraries}
library(tidyverse)  # Loads ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, stringr automatically
library(paletteer)
library(patchwork)
library(metacoder)
library(taxa)
```

```{r load-data}
# csv files as a list - can take one or more. should have standard taxonomy columns
# and then samples with whole numbers
file_paths <- c(
  "/mnt/work-drive/kira/nanopore16s/run1/final_clean_taxa_table.csv",
  "/mnt/work-drive/kira/nanopore16s/run2/final_clean_taxa_table.csv"
)

# read and merge dynamically 
data_list <- lapply(file_paths, read.csv)

# you might have to change these column names based on the database you used, look at your files
tax_cols <- c("OTU", # change this if your equivalent column is named anything else
              "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species") 
common_cols <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(data_list, colnames))
join_cols <- intersect(tax_cols, common_cols)

if (length(data_list) == 1) {
  combined_data <- data_list[[1]]
} else {
  combined_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) {
    full_join(x, y, by = join_cols, relationship = "many-to-many")
  }, data_list)
}

# Fix NAs in counts/abundance columns
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., 0)))

sample_cols <- colnames(combined_data)[sapply(combined_data, is.numeric)]
```


## fix the reordering thing

```{r taxa-filtering-and-collapse}
# ----- OPTIONS -----
# --- BIOLOGICAL FILTERS & TAXONOMY OPTIONS ---
target_tax_level       <- "Genus"     # Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus
top_n_taxa             <- 25          # top n taxa
bubble_thresh          <- 0.001       # don't plot bubbles below x % relative abundance
filter_strategy        <- "sample"    # are the top n in all samples ("global") or for each sample ("sample")?
use_relative_abundance <- TRUE        # for absolute numbers, use FALSE
within_phylum_sort     <- "abundance" # "abundance", "alphabet", "occupancy" (sort by presence/absence)

# --- NEW SORTING OPTION ---
#   TRUE  -> Sorts within Phyla first 
#   FALSE -> Sorts strictly by overall abundance across the entire plot
group_by_phylum        <- TRUE
# if group_by_phylum is FALSE, you must ALSO set facet_by_phylumn to FALSE

# --- UNCLASSIFIED TAXA CONTROLS ---
# Options: 
#   "parent_failed" -> e.g., "Bacilli Order" or "Lactobacillaceae Genus" 
#   "unclass_of"    -> e.g., "Unclassified Genus of Lactobacillaceae" 
#   "prefix"        -> e.g., "Uncl. Lactobacillaceae"
#   "suffix"        -> e.g., "Lactobacillaceae uncl."
#   "clean"         -> e.g., "Lactobacillaceae"
uncl_style             <- "parent_failed"      
fallback_label         <- "Unknown Bacteria" 

# --- OPTIONAL: METADATA SAMPLE SORTING ---
use_metadata_sort   <- TRUE              # TRUE to sort X-axis by metadata, FALSE to keep default
metadata_file       <- "/mnt/work-drive/kira/rproj-rnaseq/results2/testmeta.csv"    # Path to your metadata file
metadata_sample_col <- "Sample"        # Column in metadata matching your CSV column headers
metadata_sort_col   <- "Order"       # Column in metadata you want to sort samples by (e.g., "Day", "Group")

# -----

# --- INITIALIZE COLS TO PLOT ---
# We make a copy of sample_cols here. If metadata sorting is off, plot_cols remains untouched 
plot_cols <- sample_cols 

# Validate metadata selection immediately if active
if (use_metadata_sort) {
  if (!exists("metadata_file") || !file.exists(metadata_file)) {
    stop("Metadata file is either missing or you forgot to comment it back on")
  }
  if (!exists("metadata_sample_col") || !exists("metadata_sort_col")) {
    stop("You forgot to turn the metadata columns off.")
  }
  
  # Load metadata (auto-detects tab vs comma)
  meta_df <- read.table(metadata_file, header = TRUE, 
                        sep = ifelse(grepl("\\.tsv$|\\.txt$", tolower(metadata_file)), "\t", ","), 
                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  if (!metadata_sample_col %in% colnames(meta_df)) {
    stop(metadata_sample_col, "' is not in the metadata file.")
  }
  if (!metadata_sort_col %in% colnames(meta_df)) {
    stop("Sort column '", metadata_sort_col, "' does not exist in the metadata file.")
  }
  
  # Extract sorted sample order from metadata
  ordered_samples <- meta_df %>%
    arrange(.data[[metadata_sort_col]]) %>%
    pull(.data[[metadata_sample_col]])
  
  # Keep only samples that actually exist in our raw data's sample_cols
  ordered_samples <- ordered_samples[ordered_samples %in% sample_cols]
  
  if (length(ordered_samples) == 0) {
    stop("Ooops, there are no samples names in your metadata that match your dataframe.")
  }
  
  # Append any dataset columns that were missing from metadata
  missing_samples <- setdiff(sample_cols, ordered_samples)
  if (length(missing_samples) > 0) {
    warning("Note: ", length(missing_samples), " sample(s) were missing from metadata. Appending to the end.")
    ordered_samples <- c(ordered_samples, missing_samples)
  }
  
  # Save the finalized order to our plot variable
  plot_cols <- ordered_samples
}

# --- FIX UNCLASSIFIED TAXONOMY ---
rescue_taxonomy <- function(df, level, style = "parent_failed", fallback = "Unknown Bacteria") {
  levels_order <- c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")
  target_idx <- which(levels_order == level)
  
  tax_matrix <- as.matrix(df[, levels_order[1:target_idx], drop = FALSE])
  clean_labels <- character(nrow(df))
  
  for (r in 1:nrow(df)) {
    current_val <- tax_matrix[r, target_idx]
    
    if (is.na(current_val) || current_val == "" || str_detect(tolower(current_val), "unclassified")) {
      
      # 1. Find the FIRST level that failed (moving left-to-right)
      first_unclass_idx <- NA
      for (c in 1:target_idx) {
        val <- tax_matrix[r, c]
        if (is.na(val) || val == "" || str_detect(tolower(val), "unclassified")) {
          first_unclass_idx <- c
          break
        }
      }
      
      # 2. Grab last known parent
      resolved_name <- fallback
      if (!is.na(first_unclass_idx) && first_unclass_idx > 1) {
        for (p_idx in (first_unclass_idx - 1):1) {
          parent_val <- tax_matrix[r, p_idx]
          if (!is.na(parent_val) && parent_val != "" && !str_detect(tolower(parent_val), "unclassified")) {
            
            failed_level_name <- levels_order[first_unclass_idx]
            
            resolved_name <- switch(style,
              "parent_failed" = paste(parent_val, failed_level_name),
              "unclass_of"    = paste("Unclassified", failed_level_name, "of", parent_val), 
              "suffix"        = paste(parent_val, "uncl."),
              "prefix"        = paste("Uncl.", parent_val),
              "clean"         = parent_val,
              paste(parent_val, "uncl.") 
            )
            break
          }
        }
      }
      clean_labels[r] <- resolved_name
    } else {
      if (level %in% c("Genus", "Species")) {
        clean_labels[r] <- paste0("*", current_val, "*")
      } else {
        clean_labels[r] <- current_val
      }
    }
  }
  df$Clean_Tax_Label <- clean_labels
  return(df)
}

processed_data <- rescue_taxonomy(
  combined_data, 
  level = target_tax_level, 
  style = uncl_style, 
  fallback = fallback_label
)

# Collapse duplicates (use plot_cols to keep columns safe)
collapsed_data <- processed_data %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Clean_Tax_Label) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(plot_cols), sum), .groups = 'drop')

# convert to % if relative abundance toggled
if (use_relative_abundance) {
  collapsed_data <- collapsed_data %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(plot_cols), ~ (. / sum(.)) * 100))
  abundance_measure_text <- "Relative Abundance (%)"
  abundance_file_stamp   <- "relative"
} else {
  collapsed_data <- collapsed_data %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(plot_cols), ~ .))
  abundance_measure_text <- "Raw Read Count"
  abundance_file_stamp   <- "absolute"
}

# filter to top X taxa
if (filter_strategy == "global") {
  bubble_filtered <- collapsed_data %>%
    mutate(Total_Abundance = rowSums(across(all_of(plot_cols)))) %>%
    arrange(desc(Total_Abundance)) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n_taxa) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(plot_cols), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance")
} else {
  bubble_filtered <- collapsed_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(plot_cols), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
    group_by(Sample) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = Abundance, n = top_n_taxa, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup()
}

bubble_plot_data <- bubble_filtered %>% filter(Abundance > bubble_thresh)

# Handle phylum sorting
bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  mutate(Phylum_Total = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Clean_Tax_Label) %>%
  mutate(Taxon_Total = sum(Abundance),
         Taxon_Occupancy = sum(Abundance > 0)) %>%
  ungroup() # Corrected: Removed the stray assignment

# Sort dataframe 
# Determine sort hierarchy based on whether we are grouping by phylum
if (group_by_phylum) {
  # Sorts within Phyla first 
  if (within_phylum_sort == "abundance") {
    bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>% arrange(desc(Phylum_Total), desc(Taxon_Total))
  } else if (within_phylum_sort == "alphabet") {
    bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>% arrange(Phylum, Clean_Tax_Label)
  } else if (within_phylum_sort == "occupancy") {
    bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>% arrange(desc(Phylum_Total), desc(Taxon_Occupancy))
  }
} else {
  # Sorts globally by individual taxon abundance, ignoring phylum boundaries
  bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>% arrange(desc(Taxon_Total))
}

# Extract factors in sorted order
ordered_phyla <- unique(bubble_plot_data$Phylum)
y_levels      <- rev(unique(bubble_plot_data$Clean_Tax_Label))

# Apply the factors safely
bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = ordered_phyla),
    Clean_Tax_Label = factor(Clean_Tax_Label, levels = y_levels)
  )

# Set the factor level order for the Sample column to match plot_cols order exactly
bubble_plot_data <- bubble_plot_data %>%
  mutate(Sample = factor(Sample, levels = plot_cols))
```

Here we actually make the figures and stitch them together

```{r heat-trees-patchwork}
# ----- OPTIONS -----

# layout optoins for multi-panel heat tress
metacoder_layout  <- "horizontal"   # "vertical", "horizontal", or "grid"
grid_columns      = 2         # Only used if metacoder_layout is "grid" (set to NULL to auto-calculate)

# individual panel scaling dimensions (in inches)
panel_width       <- 8
panel_height      <- 6

# unclassified node control
remove_unclassified_nodes <- TRUE  # Set to TRUE to prune "unclassified" branches at best taxonomy

# -----

# --- HEAT TREES ---

# prepare metacoder taxonomy/truncate dead-ends 
levels_order <- c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")
target_idx   <- which(levels_order == target_tax_level)
active_ranks <- levels_order[1:target_idx]

# Map our 'Clean_Tax_Label' back to the processed data & clean names
lineage_data <- processed_data %>%
  filter(Clean_Tax_Label %in% bubble_plot_data$Clean_Tax_Label) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(active_ranks), ~ ifelse(is.na(.) | . == "" | str_detect(tolower(.), "unclassified"), 
                                               "Unclassified", .)))

# Build concatenated lineage path that dynamically DEAD-ENDS 
# at the last classified taxonomic level.
lineage_data$Full_Lineage <- apply(lineage_data[, active_ranks, drop = FALSE], 1, function(row) {
  # If the on/off switch is ON, truncate the lineage at the first "Unclassified"
  if (remove_unclassified_nodes) {
    unclass_idx <- which(row == "Unclassified")
    if (length(unclass_idx) > 0) {
      # Keep only the ranks BEFORE the first unclassified node
      valid_ranks <- row[1:(unclass_idx[1] - 1)]
      # If the very first rank (Kingdom) is unclassified, default to a fallback
      if (length(valid_ranks) == 0) {
        return("Unclassified")
      }
      return(paste(valid_ranks, collapse = ";"))
    }
  }
  # Otherwise , keep the full lineage as usual
  paste(row, collapse = ";")
})

# Collapse counts by truncated, unique lineages
metacoder_input <- lineage_data %>%
  group_by(Full_Lineage) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(plot_cols), sum), .groups = 'drop')

# --- meta your coder ---
obj <- parse_tax_data(
  metacoder_input, 
  class_cols = "Full_Lineage", 
  class_sep = ";"
)

# Calculate abundances across parent taxonomic ranks
obj$data$tax_abund <- calc_taxon_abund(obj, data = "tax_data", cols = plot_cols)

# Calculate relative abundance (percentage) if enabled in the engine
if (exists("use_relative_abundance") && use_relative_abundance) {
  # Safe R6 mutation: extract to standard data frame first to avoid R6 corruptions
  temp_abund_df <- as.data.frame(obj$data$tax_abund)
  
  for (col in plot_cols) {
    # Sum the raw input counts (where each read is represented only once)
    total_reads <- sum(metacoder_input[[col]], na.rm = TRUE) 
    
    if (total_reads > 0) {
      temp_abund_df[[col]] <- (temp_abund_df[[col]] / total_reads) * 100
    }
  }
  
  # Re-assign back safely
  obj$data$tax_abund <- tibble::as_tibble(temp_abund_df)
  
  unit_label <- "Relative Abundance (%)"
  file_stamp <- "relative"
} else {
  unit_label <- "Raw Read Count"
  file_stamp <- "absolute"
}

# Lock global scales across panels to prevent auto-scaling bias between samples
# Read: your size scaling can get super funky if you don't do this
global_max_abundance <- max(as.data.frame(obj$data$tax_abund)[, plot_cols], na.rm = TRUE)



# --- make all the panels ---
message("Generating trees for ", length(plot_cols), " samples...")

tree_panels <- lapply(plot_cols, function(sample_name) {
  
  # pull values for the loop
  sample_values <- obj$data$tax_abund[[sample_name]]
  
  heat_tree(
    obj,
    node_label            = taxon_names,                 
    node_size             = sample_values,               
    node_color            = sample_values,              
    
    node_color_range      = c("grey90", "yellow", "deepskyblue", "purple4"), 
    node_color_interval   = c(0, global_max_abundance), 
    node_size_interval    = c(0, global_max_abundance),  
    
    # Absolute physical sizing limits to avoid visual inflation in sparse plots
    node_size_range       = c(0.005, 0.08), 
    
    node_color_axis_label = sample_name,             
    node_size_axis_label  = unit_label,
    
    # --- CONTROL DECIMAL PLACES / SIGNIFICANT FIGURES HERE ---
    node_color_digits     = 2,  # Adjust this to control color legend digits
    node_size_digits      = 2,  # Adjust this to control size legend digits
    
    layout                = "reingold-tilford",                     
    initial_layout        = "reingold-tilford"
  )
})

# --- combine panels and export ---

num_samples <- length(plot_cols)

# figure out layout from passed variable
if (metacoder_layout == "vertical") {
  combined_panels_plot <- wrap_plots(tree_panels, ncol = 1) 
  

  dynamic_width  <- panel_width
  dynamic_height <- num_samples * panel_height
  layout_label   <- "vertical"
  
} else if (metacoder_layout == "horizontal") {
  combined_panels_plot <- wrap_plots(tree_panels, nrow = 1)
  
  dynamic_width  <- num_samples * panel_width
  dynamic_height <- panel_height
  layout_label   <- "horizontal"
  
} else if (metacoder_layout == "grid") {
  # If grid_columns isn't specified, calculate a balanced square-ish layout
  if (is.null(grid_columns)) {
    grid_columns <- ceiling(sqrt(num_samples))
  }
  grid_rows <- ceiling(num_samples / grid_columns)
  
  combined_panels_plot <- wrap_plots(tree_panels, ncol = grid_columns)
  
  # Both width and height scale based on grid dimensions
  dynamic_width  <- grid_columns * panel_width
  dynamic_height <- grid_rows * panel_height
  layout_label   <- paste0("grid_", grid_columns, "x", grid_rows)
  
} else {
  # Fallback to single column if a typo is made
  warning("Invalid layout choice. Defaulting to vertical stack.")
  combined_panels_plot <- wrap_plots(tree_panels, ncol = 1) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect")
  dynamic_width  <- panel_width
  dynamic_height <- num_samples * panel_height
  layout_label   <- "vertical_fallback"
}

# Dynamic file name
date_stamp <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")
unclass_stamp <- if (remove_unclassified_nodes) "truncated" else "full_lineage"
matrix_output_filename <- paste0(
  "heattree_panels_", layout_label, "_",
  target_tax_level, "_", 
  filter_strategy, "_top", 
  top_n_taxa, "_", 
  unclass_stamp, "_",
  file_stamp, "_", 
  date_stamp, ".png"
)

# save image
ggsave(
  filename = matrix_output_filename, 
  plot     = combined_panels_plot, 
  width    = dynamic_width, 
  height   = dynamic_height, 
  dpi      = 300,
  limitsize = FALSE # Safe-guard for massive multi-panel outputs
)

# Success message
message("Huzzah! Metacoder ", metacoder_layout, " panel plot saved as: ", matrix_output_filename)
```
